channel.title: Game Development on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Game Development on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Game Development As An Identity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development as an identity And

item.title: Brainstorming Example
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to do a brainstorming example.

item.title: Game Development As A Family Car Trip
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game development.

item.title: Zen And Game Development
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Zen and game development.

item.title: Staying Healthy While Developing Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about recent findings in fossilized endothermic theorapsids.

item.title: "Impossible" Ideas
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about impossible ideas.

item.title: Hypothetical Game Jam
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a hypothetical game, Jam.

item.title: How Much Devs Should Play
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how much devs should play.

item.title: Great External Ideas
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about great external ideas, and by that I mean ideas that came from outside the development team, that were really good and worked and ended up in the game.

item.title: Playing Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about playing games now, probably one of the most frequent questions or question types I get on this channel.

item.title: Game Development Roles
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development roles.

item.title: A Non-Game Design Spec
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a noname design spec.

item.title: Game Development Tunnel Vision
item.description: Sal SE, Tim, par gotcha. I bet you right now some of you are reaching for that YouTube translate button and some of you were thinking, whoa, Tim’s French accent is terrible, trust me, that’s been said. No, I’m doing this video about game development, tunnel vision, because it’s one of the things that occurred to me. I get a lot of questions about a lot of things and early on, when there were less of you, I would answer and say, oh, that’s not something I know or I can’t talk about it I did do a video on things I can’t or won’t answer, but since then there’s been a lot more questions that Encompass a lot more things, even about game development or tangentially related game development, that I literally cannot answer. I either don’t know it or I’m not qualified to answer.

item.title: The Business Side Of Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the business side of game development.

item.title: Idea Battles
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about idea battles.

item.title: Research
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about research, specifically the research that you do when you first start a game.

item.title: The "Best" OS For Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the best operating system to make games.

item.title: Scenes From A Life Of Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to show you scenes from a life spent in game development.

item.title: Game Design Specification Sections
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about all the different design specifications that a single game may need, because about a month ago I gave an example of how to write a design specification and gypsum generation asks: what are all the design specifications that a game might need?

item.title: Writing Design Specifications
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about how to write design specifications.

item.title: Stop Making Excuses
item.description: It’s me, Tim, and it’s tough love today on the Canon games Channel because I want all of you to stop making excuses and start making games.

item.title: Fast Fail
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about a development methodology called Fast fail.

item.title: Game Development Caution, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to do part two of game development caution, because that video got a lot of views and a lot of comments and I kind of want to talk about it.

item.title: Let's Talk About Ideas
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk to you about ideas.

item.title: My University Game Development Class
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about my university class in game design.

item.title: School As Preparation For Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about school as preparation for game development.

item.title: Game Development Caution
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about something that, for a lack of a better phrase, I’m going to call game development caution.

item.title: My Bad Game Development Skills
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development tasks that I’m bad at.

item.title: Features: From Ideas To Implementation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from Wyatt’s Vlog who asks: could you talk about the steps you take when exploring and implementing a feature?

item.title: Game Development Time
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game development time.

item.title: Game Development: Then vs. Now
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to ask a question from, or answer a question — I keep doing that from — Jeremy Mason 9802 asks: do you think game development is harder or Worse nowadays compared to, say, the 90s, or is it better or the same?

item.title: Brainstorming
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about brainstorming, basically my thoughts on it, how we did it, what worked, what didn’t.

item.title: Learning Lessons From Failure
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, so I’ve been telling a lot of stories.

item.title: How To Write Design Docs
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. I’ve told a lot of stories about game development, but today I actually want to talk about process, specifically how I make my design documents.
